title: Date-brittle tests: solution_lesson_distiller hardcodes 'recent' dates that rot
what_failed: TestCheckRecency::test_recent_passes and TestRunCheck::test_healthy_solutions_passes assert Status.PASS using the hardcoded date '2026-05-10T00:00:00Z'. RECENCY_DAYS=30; as of 2026-06-11 that date is 32 days old, so check_recency correctly returns WARN and the tests fail. The checker is right; the tests rotted.
where_failed: Hardcoded ISO timestamps in test fixtures that are compared against datetime.now() in the code under test.
why_likely: Tests written 2026-05 used a then-recent literal date instead of computing one relative to now. Classic time-bomb test — guaranteed to fail exactly RECENCY_DAYS after authoring.
fix_plan: Replace hardcoded 'recent' dates with datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1), formatted ISO, in both failing tests.
Grep the rest of testing/ for the same pattern (hardcoded 2026-* dates asserted as 'recent') and fix any others found in the same commit.
